segments.text: The Lord said to Moses,
Speak to the priests, the sons of Aaron, and say to them: 'A priest must not make himself ceremonially unclean for any of his people who die,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: except for a close relative, such as his mother or father, his son or daughter, his brother,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: or an unmarried sister who is dependent on him since she has no husband—for her he may make himself unclean.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: He must not make himself unclean for people related to him by marriage, and so defile himself.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Priests must not shave their heads or shave off the edges of their beards or cut their bodies.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: They must be holy to their God and must not profane the name of their God. Because they present the food offerings to the Lord, the food of their God, they are to be holy.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: They must not marry women defiled by prostitution or divorced from their husbands, because priests are holy to their God.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: Regard them as holy, because they offer up the food of your God. Consider them holy, because I the Lord am holy—I who make you holy.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: If a priest's daughter defiles herself by becoming a prostitute, she disgraces her father; she must be burned in the fire.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The high priest, the one among his brothers who has had the anointing oil poured on his head and who has been ordained to wear the priestly garments, must not let his hair become unkempt or tear his clothes.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: He must not enter a place where there is a dead body. He must not make himself unclean, even for his father or mother,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: nor leave the sanctuary of his God or desecrate it, because he has been dedicated by the anointing oil of his God. I am the Lord.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The woman he marries must be a virgin.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: He must not marry a widow, a divorced woman, or a woman defiled by prostitution, but only a virgin from his own people,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: so that he will not defile his offspring among his people. I am the Lord, who makes him holy.'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: The Lord said to Moses,
Say to Aaron: 'For the generations to come none of your descendants who has a defect may come near to offer the food of his God.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: No man who has any defect may come near: no man who is blind or lame, disfigured or deformed;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: no man with a crippled foot or hand,
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: or who is a hunchback or a dwarf, or who has any eye defect, or who has festering or running sores or damaged testicles.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: No descendant of Aaron the priest who has any defect is to come near to present the food offerings to the Lord. He has a defect; he must not come near to offer the food of his God.
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: He may eat the most holy food of his God, as well as the holy food;
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: yet because of his defect, he must not go near the curtain or approach the altar, and so desecrate my sanctuary. I am the Lord, who makes them holy.'
segments.speaker: The Lord

segments.text: So Moses told this to Aaron and his sons and to all the Israelites.
